text: Fine tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) for target tasks such as sentiment analysis and product description generation requires large amounts of computational resources and domain specific knowledge of the fine tuning approaches for achieving desired performance. Thus fine tuning LLMs is not efficient for over 100 models. LLAMAFACTORY is an open source, general-purpose, easy-to-use toolkit that eases the processing for large amounts of LLMs and their efficient fine tuning. We integrate a set of state-of-the-art optimizations (i.e., LoRA, GaLore, and DoRA) along with training modes such as supervised fine-tuning (SFT), direct preference optimization (DPO), and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). Additionally, we propose a zero-code web interface named as LLAMABOARD. This interface allows users to set up their fine-tuning tasks and model outputs by showing the whole fine-tuning process with real-time updates, eliminating the need to write codes to view the performance of different LLMs and their fine-tuned models. Experiments show that the usage of LLAMAFACTORY leads to the reduction of memory consumption and the maintaining of high-throughput while achieving better results in both language modeling future language and text generation tasks. The community adopted this fantastic platform for many applications and studies of LLMs.
